Love the game but can't recommend it. The devs released a half-finished game even though there were multiple playtests for them to realize the game isn't ready yet. A lot of server issues that make the game almost more rng based than skill based. The other multiplayer game they made a few years ago had the exact same issues, so it seems they don't learn from their mistakes.

It's been a few months since I posted the section above in this review, and the game still has the same issues. The player base is dwindling and the game is currently in the dying phase, which is kind of sad considering how it's only been a few months since its release. Maybe we'll get Sifu 2 once this gets abandoned.

Após 200 horas de jogo depois de alcançar o elo mais alto do jogo posso dar uma review boa.


Rematch é um jogo incompleto feito por uma empresa inexperiente com o gênero, que ainda precisa de meses ou anos para tornar o jogo em uma experiência consistente, dito isso o centro do jogo é divertido, viciante e permite uma experiência única.

Os jogadores que se apaixonarem com a gameplay irão deixar de lado os diversos problemas de performance, netcode, matchmaking e entre outros. Porém, o jogador casual provavelmente irá se frustrar rapidamente e com razão.

Rematch não será o próximo gigante do E-SPORT nesse estado, mas ele tem potencial para envelhecer com vinho ou como leite, é necessário cuidado pela parte da desenvolvedora para consertar rapidamente os problemas e continuar a dar suporte ao jogo.

O jogo com randoms se resume a basicamente em "Relaxe e confia no pai, se liga na play" e seu time toma o gol, felizmente possuo um grupo de amigos focados em fazer gols e passes, por mais que sejam gols contra e passes para o time adversário, ainda confio no meu time e creio que algum dia sairemos do bronze div 3.

Antes de tudo, gostaria de falar que esse jogo é MUITO BOM. Uma premissa sólida e bem feita tanto mecanicamente quanto graficamente, com um bônus de ser "inovador" no mercado. É divertido, não possui um sistema de microtransações agressivo, a playerbase (ainda) não está tão tóxica e ousadamente digo que tem futuro até mesmo no cenário de E-sports. Porém, Todavia Estrondosa, os servidores estão uma porcaria, com Laggings ridículos e até mesmo Desconexões Forçadas, e um PREÇO que NÃO CONDIZ com a situação atual do game. Tenho muita esperança para ele ainda, e espero que não seja em vão, mas a indústria já me decepciona há um tempo, e isso cansa não só a mim, mas a todos aqueles que procuram o mínimo de decência num produto vendido em sua versão final.

5v5でそのうちの選手一人を担当するPvPオンラインサッカーゲーム。ファウルなし、オフサイドなし、ボールアウトなしで得点が決まるまではノンストップという何でもありのルールが特徴。開発は『Sifu』で知られるSloclap。

基本的に上に書いたコンセプト以上のむずかしいことはなにもない。
シュート時のエイミングのシステムがTPSっぽいとか、その影響でパッド操作派とキーボード＆マウス操作派に分かれているとかはあるのだけれど、そんなのは些事だ。

そのシンプルさゆえに、この『REMATCH』はプレイヤーたちにもっとも困難な要求を課してくる。

「サッカーをやること」だ。

試合がキックオフした瞬間、このゲームは猛烈な勢いで「サッカー」としての形を崩していきつづける。それはシステムのせいではなく、人のせいだ。ずっとボールを持ち続けてパスを一切ださない、なんてのは序の口で、ディフェンスをしない、ゴールキーパーに入らない、パスをあさっての方向にミスる、ドリブルの仕方がわからない、次にどこにどういうボールが来るか見越して行動できない、敵がミスると煽る、どころか、味方がミスすると煽る。
試合終了の六分ちょうどまで、ただひたすらチームの調和が乱れていく。

そう、このゲームの大半のプレイヤーはサッカーをチームスポーツだと知らないのだ。
あなたは、このゲームの必勝法は「サッカー」の形を六分間保ち続けることだと気づくだろう。
だが、今度はそれが次なる障壁になっていく。
気づいてしまうのだ。
自分もまた「サッカー」を壊してしまう一要因なのである、と。
理解があっても技術が追いつかず、技術が追いついても常に正解をたたき出せるかはわからない。とくにソロだろ毎回プレイスタイルの違うプレイヤーのガチャになるため、前回正解だったプレイが今回は大惨事を引き起こすことだって少なくない。
あるいはエリート帯と呼ばれる楽園には「サッカー」を理解し技術に習熟した天使たちがあなたを迎えて、未来永劫楽しい「サッカー」を保証してくれるかもしれない。いつか、いつかは。ただ、それは今ではない。シルバー帯やゴールド帯で血反吐をはきながら自分自身も景品となっているチームメイトガチャに這いずりまわっている今このときではない。

『REMATCH』では、「サッカー」は壊れる運命にある。
勝つチームが相手チームより優れたチームなのではない。
負けるチームが相手よりひどいチームであるだけだ。そういう摂理でできている。
そして、ひどいチームではだれもが「なんで自分以外全員へたくそなんだ」とキレている。指揮者不在のオーケストラのような険悪さ。


野良を、ソロをオススメする。
顔見知りと固定メンツなんて組むな。そんなものは週末のぬるい社会人フットサルサークルと変わらない。現実にあるんだから現実でやれ。健康的になれるぞ。
『REMATCH』の真価は、互いの顔も素性も言葉もわからない相手（味方）との本気の罵り合いにこそある。その先にある万に一つの勝利にある。
六分間の地獄を乗り越え「Your Team WIN」と表示された瞬間、すべてのチームメイトへの憎しみが裏返り、全員の顔が天使に見えてくる。
エリート帯へなど行く必要はもはやない。
今いるランクがわたしたちの天国であり、これがサッカーだ。

本物のサッカーがここにはある。

Really fun with a solid foundation in its mechanics. But it can't get a "Recommend" until they fix the server/lag, which is persistent in every single game. It's not just the lag that occurs after ever single goal. It's the animation & sound queues that make it seem like you made a save but apparently didn't, or the tackles that seemingly give you a steal but end up being awarded to the opponent who's already 20 feet past you.

If/when the lag is fixed, it's an easy "Recommend" for me.

I've played in the beta and the issues from there are still prevailing in the final game. Its a real shame. I haven't been excited in a new game for long and I even bought the version for 10 extra bucks to play it earlier. I was prepared for it not being perfect, but I was expecting more responsive devs as this is a new game alot of people have been hyped for for quite some time. 
The first days we're fun, met alot of people which made it fun to play. 
But if you play this game by yourself you get the same experience as getting dragged through glass shards by the balls. The community has rotten very fast, i rarely have wholesome encounters in the game chat. People abuse the quickchat to basically flame you if they're balls havent dropped yet to use a microphone. Most of the time you get people calling out slurs so might aswell just play with no Chat if you want to save some braincells.
I really hope the developers fix the Issues in this game (can't be arsed too write them down. But one of them being the goalkeeper losing posession of the ball right after picking it up, or simply losing priority to it. Which is gamechanging and should be fixed).
Also no report feature, the battlepass is locked on a timer and ranks don't matter since you get matched with the whole spectrum of ranks. 
Sad to leave a bad review but I simply lost the fun in it.

I want to love this game, I really do.

This is such a fresh entry in football, the mechanics are super unique and thoughtful especially how you need to actually control dribbling and tap a button to tap the ball when running; it opens the game up to so much mechanical creativity and skill expression.

But this game unfortunately is on unreal engine, it crashed in the middle of a ranked game for EVERYONE in the match. Once I rejoin, we went from 1-1 to 1-4 because one person rejoined the quickest and got to score 3 times before we rejoined.

There are also some obvious jank that somehow did not get picked up during play testing, like no retry button on the training grounds, some of the menus are awful to use, the game never seems to be accurate where it tells me I'm the captain of the team before proceeding to not use my stadium and AR at all.

There are other gameplay issues too, some have been fixed like the permanent hovering with headers; while others still remain like GK's forced into a dive animation which takes them out of the penalty box allowing any striker to steal the ball out of their hands with a kick that the GK cant react to because they're still locked in an animation.

There's also no player reporting or any other mechanic like that, so it's frustrating when you have some frustrating individuals who either troll or just throw games and can't do anything about.

This game has so much potential, and personally I think the battle pass is very generous for people who haven't bought it; although it does urk me so much of it is locked in a paywall for a game you have to buy.

The devs clearly had a vision and passion for the game, but maybe lacked the game dev experience to perfect the game.

I hope this is resolved asap, it would be a shame to lose such a fresh idea.

[h2] 4 Min Video Review [/h2]
https://youtu.be/ocqPvFqBdJ4

[h2] Written Review [/h2]
Rematch is a much more personal take on football than we see in most games, with a traditional third person camera perspective, fewer players on the field, and the ability to only control one person.

[h3] What is it? [/h3]
As much of a meme it is to say the game feels like Rocket league but with feet... it's true. The general flow of the game is really similar. But since its by the devs who brought us Sifu, the game also has a few more layers of complexity.

Not only does the game require some strategic and quick thinking, but also a bit of mechanical skill.

[h3] Gameplay Loop [/h3]
Firstly, when shooting the ball, you need to manually aim it with your crosshair. You can add a curve, which feels so good when you manage to trick a goalie with it.

There's also a separate pass button that hits the ball much faster, and is not aimed with your crosshair, but instead the direction you input at the moment, allowing for some stylish backwards passes.

Your final main action is your dribbling button, which obviously allows you to avoid tackles, maintain posession of the ball, or even push the ball ahead of you so you can run at your full speed without being slowed down.

[h3] Depth [/h3]
The game has a bunch of other smaller mechanics that make it feel much more rewarding like: 
A modifier button that turns all dribbles and passes into aerial variants, numerous ways to tackle and intercept the ball depending on the situation, a stamina system that you need to lightly manage, ensuring you have enough for those play-making moments. Finally there are also short windows of time where you can pass or shoot the ball before its even fully reached you, if you're fast enough.

All of this adds so much to the game, and although it may sound a bit daunting at first, you get the hang of it relatively quickly, and there are a bunch of tutorials and workshops you can use to improve.

[h3] Goalie [/h3]
If you're on goalie duty... it can be quite stressful, but also so rewarding if you make the save when all eyes are on you. Because of all of the tools at your opponents' disposal, you'll need to stay alert, and choose the right way to intercept the ball. Whether it be leaping towards a curved ball, or simply snatching it from their possession.

Strangely enough, it can sometimes feel like a fighting or dueling game like For Honor, where you have to read your opponents. It feels great to get better in this role.

[h3] Controls [/h3]
For the most part, I think the controls are quite intuitive, but they're not perfect. It's relatively comfortable on a controller, but turning around to make quick shots can feel a bit cumbersome.

Keyboard & mouse brings that precision, but suffers from a few other issues like unusual keybindings and is prone to accidental passing until you get used to it, or they add more options. (Which they have said they will be.)
In the end, I felt the best control scheme was kb&m with the thumb buttons preset.

[h3] Game Modes [/h3]
You have the choice between 3v3, 4v4, 5v5.

To me, the game is at its best with fewer players on the field, because it feels most unique then, and really allows mechanical skills and thoughtful teamwork to shine. But I'm also someone who always preferred duos in Rocket League too.
The bigger modes are fine too, but feel a bit less interesting. 
Of course, if you're not playing with friends, you need to be ready for selfish players that will beg for the ball, but never pass it back to you, or even outright leave their goal posts, but there's not much the game can do about that.

[h3] Progression & Customization [/h3]
As you play, you will earn in-game XP to level your battle pass, and currency that you can use to purchase things from the store. Progression speed is okay, but at the moment there are really not many options to choose from, so for a while, get used to seeing similar looking characters.

One of the game's customization options, is the option to change the AR field surrounding players, allowing the game to take place in a jungle or desert for example, depending on if Home or Away is leading. And they bring their own unique music too. I love these maps, they're beautiful, and really help make the game feel unique.
I feel like it's a shame to lock them behind progression, as they add more variety and uniqueness to the game.

[h3] Conclusion [/h3]
Overall though, the game is fantastic. It's managed to hook me and my partner like only Rocket League did, despite the fact that neither of us are interested in football.
Slowly getting more comfortable with your skills is such a great feeling, and pulling off tightly strung assists with your teammates is so rewarding. Pick it up if this sounds fun to you!

Great game, bugs to be fixed but what game doesn't have some bugs to be work on. My take on where the game needs serious improvement. Most importantly the ranking system needs an overhaul. Its a very basic system which is nice but i do believe you should lose less and gain more when based on performance. When you get to the next rank there is no de-ranking system which is part of the problem with the basic ranking system, with the people that get carried by their friends smurfs, the ranking system is the biggest issue apart from bugs. Solo players suffer the most because of that. I don't think there should be a minimap in a ranked gamemode, it's incredibly nice but it makes the game a bit easier and in ranked you should rely on callouts or just your eyes (maybe you don't have to take in completely away but should be smaller, i should see only whats around me and not half the map). As someone that basically only plays ranked, there needs to be a better system in place. 

It's currently not worth 30$ but if it goes on sale for 15$ or cheaper it'd be much more worthwhile but at the same time most people are willing to pay 70$ for EA or 2k sports game every year so 30$ really ain't much in hindsight.

Now onto the why I do recommend this to a competitive gamer or someone that just likes to play pro-clubs or even for someone that is just a football/soccer fan.

The customization system is nice and the concept of the game is nice, yea its just a soccer game, you run on a field, pass the ball, shoot the ball, save goals as a keeper but the difference from clubs is its smaller and you have freedom to play the way you want too, everyone has the same skill-set and no one has an advantage over the other player (other than latency). It's just all about how you use those skills you have and how much better you are mentally than the other players on the field, its not a solo game, with all the few skills we're given you can't do it all by yourself and as frustrating as that can be, it's also one of the greatest feeling to get good chemistry with down with your buddies or even more satisfying getting good chemistry with randoms. The audio is pretty good, I can tell how far or close someone is based on the sound. With their plans for the future of the game, it's a game worth backing and I recommend you try it if you're into a soccer style of play.

Rematch avait tout pour plaire : un concept prometteur, un gameplay compétitif dynamique, et une communauté passionnée prête à s’investir. Mais à mesure que le jeu avance, un constat s’impose : l’expérience est entachée par de nombreux bugs persistants, et surtout, par un manque flagrant de transparence et de communication de la part des développeurs.

❌ Des bugs toujours présents... et ignorés
Depuis plusieurs mises à jour, de nombreux joueurs signalent des bugs récurrents :

Problèmes de matchmaking

Freeze en pleine partie

Désynchronisation des scores

Bugs de hitbox ou d’animation
Malheureusement, ces bugs sont rarement reconnus officiellement, encore moins corrigés rapidement. Les patch notes sont souvent vagues, et la communauté se sent abandonnée, livrée à elle-même, entre frustration et déception.

📢 Une communication trop centrée sur la boutique
Ce qui agace encore plus les joueurs, c’est que la communication du studio semble de plus en plus tournée vers la boutique. Skins, packs, offres temporaires… Les réseaux sociaux et annonces officielles mettent l’accent sur les éléments monétisables, plutôt que sur les correctifs attendus ou les évolutions du gameplay.

Ce choix donne une impression claire : les priorités ne sont plus l’expérience de jeu, mais la rentabilité. Et cela se ressent dans l’engagement des joueurs, qui diminue peu à peu.

🗣️ Une communauté en demande, mais peu écoutée
Les forums, Discord, et autres plateformes regorgent de retours constructifs. La communauté ne demande pas la perfection, mais un dialogue honnête, des réponses claires, et une vraie feuille de route. Ce silence radio sur les problèmes techniques crée une cassure entre les développeurs et les joueurs.

⚠️ En conclusion
Rematch est un jeu au fort potentiel, mais il est actuellement freiné par :

Des bugs récurrents non corrigés

Une communication quasi inexistante sur les problèmes

Une priorité donnée au contenu payant plutôt qu’à la qualité de jeu

Les joueurs méritent mieux qu’un jeu qui semble les ignorer. Le studio a encore le temps de redresser la barre, mais cela passera par une reconnexion sincère avec sa communauté. Sans ça, Rematch risque de perdre peu à peu ce qui faisait sa force : ses joueurs.

✏️ Édit – Avis actuel sur Rematch après les dernières mises à jour

Depuis les dernières mises à jour, Rematch a clairement connu des améliorations notables. Plusieurs problèmes qui rendaient l’expérience frustrante ont été corrigés : certains freezes sont bien moins fréquents, le matchmaking semble globalement plus stable, et quelques bugs visuels gênants ont disparu. Ces correctifs apportent un vrai confort de jeu par rapport aux débuts.

Cependant, le tableau n’est pas encore idéal. Si certains soucis ont été résolus, d’autres continuent de hanter les parties. On note surtout :

des désynchronisations qui réapparaissent par moments,

des bugs de hitbox ou d’animations qui persistent encore selon les situations,

et parfois même le retour de problèmes qu’on pensait réglés.

L’expérience de jeu reste donc en dents de scie : meilleure qu’avant, mais encore loin d’être totalement fluide.

⚖️ Bilan actuel
Rematch avance, c’est indéniable. Le jeu est plus jouable qu’à ses débuts et certains correctifs ont réellement fait du bien. Mais la stabilité globale reste fragile, avec cette impression que chaque mise à jour règle un point tout en en faisant ressurgir un autre.
En résumé : du progrès, mais un terrain encore instable, qui empêche le potentiel du jeu de s’exprimer pleinement.

I was on a soccer team growing up and always hated FIFA. It really didn't hit the nerves like this game does. You can be an amazing player, but it's the teamwork as a whole that carries the game. There are a few flaws here and there, but Sloclap is a company you can trust. Absolver (one of their previous games) was supported all the way to the bitter end. Even when things weren't looking great, they released a free expansion for the few players left (it was peak). 90% of the cosmetics can be unlocked just by playing the game, and I’m not talking about a 30-day grind for a new color. You can earn so many credits that you genuinely have nothing left to buy, from Hair styles, Jersey styles, shin guards, animations to celebrate your goals definitely a breath of fresh air with how the gaming industry has been recently.

If you have never played a sports game you don't have to worry. Its definitely easy to learn but hard to master and soccer knowledge definitely helps but is not needed in order to get better at the game. 

Sports game of the year, Sloclap have done it again.

It was fun at first, but after playing it for a week, I can no longer ignore the game's glaring issues.
First, I've never played a competitive multiplayer game with server issue this bad, every match always have random packet loss, ping spike, enormous amount of rubber banding, random players getting disconnected mid game, and the most noticeable players that keep failing to join a game for no apparent reason. 
The players, my God every ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ in this game think they're Blue Lock MC incarnation, Ronaldo's second coming, or The Next Messi, they never pass, always shoot everytime they got the ball, but funnily enough they never score for ♥♥♥♥. Always rainbow flick, play with the ball and lost it near the penalty box, always chasing the ball even though there's already another teammate that's pressing the ball, resulting in ABUNDANT amount of space in the back line, and so on and so on.
The gameplay, being a keeper here sucks, probably the closest you can get in the amount of pain to getting kicked in the balls. You correctly guessed the ball's trajectory, dove into the ball, got into the catching animation, and suddenly the animation got cancelled, you lost the ball, conceded the goal, and your good for nothing teammates spam "Good Job". Yup sounds about right. The ranked queue system also sucks ass, since you can't get demoted you always play the same guy 7/10 times, even when they're way above or below your rank, basically balance doesn't exist here.
I'mm done with this game for now, until they fix the game BIG issues.

As someone who has been playing and following the game since its public beta, I can confidently say that this is one of the strangest management processes I’ve seen in a launch—full of poor decisions and a complete disregard for community feedback. I understand they’re trying to pull this off with a small team, and I know making an online game at this scale isn’t easy. But that’s no excuse for the communication gap between the players and the dev team, or for the poorly managed process overall.

From the start, it was questionable that a game with a beta attended by 60,000 players would move to full release in under two months. Strangely, the game didn’t even release as Early Access, despite its obvious lack of polish. To make things worse, after adding Game Pass, they flooded the game with premium skins and cosmetic bundles within the first month—while selling the game for $15 in Turkey and $25 elsewhere. This created a really negative impression for many players, since even the most basic functions of the game weren’t working properly at that point.

-The long-requested crossplay feature, discussed since beta, was only announced two days ago as “coming in September.” If you type “crossplay” in their Discord, a bot auto-replies instantly. It’s basically a form of indirect censorship.

-Servers don’t work properly in many regions: high ping, unfindable matches, etc.

-For a competitive, football-themed game, not having a report system is baffling. Every match, you’re forced to deal with players blaming their life failures on the goalkeeper missing a save. Now they’ve said they’ll add a report feature later.

-Matchmaking is utterly broken. In ranked games, you might get a total newbie on your team while facing opponents with 300+ hours of playtime.

-Watching the ball literally pass through your character while dribbling isn’t uncommon.

There are probably many more issues I’m forgetting or unaware of. In their post two days ago, they addressed most of these problems, promising fixes within 6-9 weeks. Naturally, this only sparked more frustration. Everyone has been aware of these issues since day one—so why wasn’t anything done earlier? Why did it take a full month just to acknowledge them publicly?

By the way, in that same post, they claimed they pushed hotfixes almost daily during the first two weeks. I don’t remember anything like that happening, and the game’s update log doesn’t show it either.

Sloclap took a game with massive potential and is ruining it with terrible management decisions. I’m not even sure they realize how much they’re damaging their own brand. If you’re considering buying this game, I strongly recommend waiting a few months. At least then it might actually be enjoyable to play.

《开球！REMATCH》是一款由师父团队制作的无规则足球游戏，说实话当时我看到预告说《师父》那家拳拳到肉的工作室宣布做足球游戏时，我是相当震惊，功夫？足球？体验以后发现，开球这部作品，用不太“足球”的方式，献上了一场纯粹的足球盛宴

与传统的足球游戏不同，在这款游戏中你看不到那些熟悉的人物面孔，不凭借球员版权吸引人，且游戏改变了传统的11v11，改为3v3，4v4，5v5的对抗比赛，相比来说场地缩小，节奏更加快速，非常适合抽空来踢一把

玩家可以通过不同按键组合实现盘带、传球等操作，还能做出彩虹过人等花式动作，称之为“足球员模拟器”也不为过。游戏真正的革命性玩法在于无规则。传统足球游戏追求拟真效果，而在开球这里，彻底摒弃越位、犯规、红黄牌等概念，甚至移除了边界线。说到边界线，游戏处于的场地边缘有类似结界的东西，一切比赛都在这个结界里进行，正因为是无规则，踢球自然也没有出界这一说，在这里完全不用有踢到观众席的顾虑，因为边界会帮你阻挡一切，甚至可以将足球反射到场上接着踢，完全可以利用这套机制靠着边界的反射来进球

也正因为是无规则，所以即使你不是很了解足球的规则，也是可以入手这款游戏的，凭借开局的新手教程再加以练习足够打出帅气的操作。但毕竟是真人联机游戏，在这种无规则的场地下，一旦有人接到球就会演变成一堆人铲你的情况，实现“少林足球”的场景也不是梦，这可能是唯一允许你“合理”滑铲拦截别人的足球游戏了

游戏为了限制也加入了体力条系统，减少了一堆人猛冲追球的情况，反而更注重团队协作，体力分配的合理安排，毕竟你也不希望好不容易到自己脚下的球因为跑不快被人抢走吧

虽然游戏的受众变广了，不太了解规则的足球小白也可以游玩，但游戏的操作门槛并没有太降低。一旦球传入你的脚下，为了应对对手的各种攻击，你需要熟练掌握按键来控住你的球。团队协作自然是重中之重，一个人的能力有限，即使是大佬在面对围攻也会有马失前蹄的情况，有效的传球能帮助团队更好的获胜

如果你有朋友一起联机游玩再好不过，野排的话很有可能出现一人单打独斗的情况，确实会有点搞心态，再加上游戏需要开加速器游玩，这也是多人联机游戏面临的一些问题。除此之外，感觉游戏目前最需要改进的就是加入人机角色，真人匹配一局难免有退出的，如果没有电脑及时补位，会造成比赛的严重不平衡，也希望官方能在后续加入电脑角色，即使是不想真人匹配的也能有所体验

但不管怎样，如果你厌倦了公式化的年货足球，渴望一片无拘无束的绿茵战场，不妨来尝试一下这个，别忘了，真正的胜利永远属于愿意打出精妙配合的团队

Here's the TL;DR : I spent $60+ on an incomplete.nearly unplayable game with great potential. I dare say I spent $60+ for an alpha test. REMATCH as of 12/7/2025 feels so incomplete that it feels like an alpha test to me.

If you like paying money to waste hours of your life playing an incomplete. buggy, laggy game. This is one of the games of all time.

Check the updates tab on steam before making a decision on it. Give this game a few months to 2-3 years at least before buying it. It'll either be complete and good by then or dead in the ditch, whichever comes first. 

Look for another football video game in the meantime or give up on football video games and play something else. or play actual football and touch some grass in the process. It costs less than this game and is healthier for you mentally and physically.




Here's a more detailed list of the gripes and issues I have with this game:


I paid $29.99 + tax + buying the game for a friend in order to get :

- A game with no player reporting system, with a community filled with short tempered losers that will own goal any match that doesn't go their way.
- A netcode that is half baked, which caused tons of missed balls and undeserved goals and losses. This is still the case as of 19/7/2025

Also, as someone who lives in the US and whose friends are all in EU, playing on EU servers makes these netcode issues incredibly common and incredibly frustrating, to the point that certain actions feel like a waste of time due to them NOT working 90% of the time. Actions such as tackling the freaking ball and jumping to catch a ball as a goalkeeper...... which are some of the most basic actions you WILL have to do in 99.9999% of matches you play.

- No cross-play, even though they said cross-play will be available at launch. I am not affected by this personally, but I already know some poor fool bought this game day 1 on PS5 thinking cross-play is available.  Good luck refunding games on the PS store.

- No option to hide quick chats that are spammed for toxicity such as "Good Job". majority of the time people spam "Good Job" to be toxic, as of 12/7/2025 you can mute the audio but you can't get rid of the text which kind of defeats the point of ignoring these pointless rage baiting messages.

- No FSR and no XESS upscalers available although NVIDIA DLSS is available day 1. You shouldn't need upscalers on a game like this anyways but this adds to the list of annoyances to me and adds to the frustration. 

- Most games infested by crop top wearing, burn scar covered, feminine sounding men with afros dyed in clown colors. If you are into that, more power to you. As for me, this reminds me too much of FC25 and FC24......I hate both those games.

- Game crashes leading into lost ranked matches. What is PC gaming nowadays without games crashing and costing you RP amirite???

- Inability to stop matchmaking after starting it..... you guys seriously couldn't be bothered to add a stop matchmaking button that works ?

I got robbed of $60 and 36+ hours on this "game". If you want to get robbed too be my guest. If not, steer clear from this mess of a game and come back in a few months to a few years.

It’s tough to be writing this. I've poured over 80 hours into Rematch (Not included are all the betas), and I genuinely believe the core of this game is something special. The controls, the depth, and those moments where a perfect play comes together… that’s the magic. You can clearly see the legendary soccer game it’s trying to be.

But honestly, the frustration is starting to win out over the fun. The game is held back by some deep-rooted problems with its fundamentals. My main frustration is with the hit registration during challenges. There are just too many moments where the outcome feels wrong. It’s best described as a feeling that the ball itself sometimes doesn’t know which player actually hit it, leading to a loss of possession that feels completely undeserved. When the result of a crucial challenge feels random instead of skill based, it just drains the motivation to improve.

Then there’s the ranked matchmaking, which can be a total crapshoot. You get thrown into these wildly unbalanced matches that kill any sense of fair competition or progress. Add the occasional server lag on top of everything else, and the whole experience just feels shaky.

It's really this mix—the unreliable hit registration, the questionable matchmaking, and an overall lack of polish—that's pushing me away. It leaves you with the nagging feeling that the game just needed more time to sort out these core problems.

So, with a heavy heart, I'm uninstalling for now. I truly hope the devs keep working on it, because I would love to come back to the polished game Rematch deserves to be. But until then, I just can't recommend it.

This game is incredibly fun, and I enjoy the concept/gameplay immensely, however, I cannot in good conscience give it a positive review. There are so many problems and design decisions that force me to inform you that the game is not worth it's price tag, some deliberate. 

Like many others have mentioned the netcode and/or servers are absolutely deplorable, and almost every single match will be fairly laggy to some degree, sometimes even to the point of making the game borderline unplayable on occasion. For example, It's not uncommon to snatch the ball from an enemy, watch it roll out behind them, then run to take possession of said ball only to watch it vanish before your eyes, that same enemy now running along with it as if nothing had happened. Goals can sometimes be scored even if the goalie is holding the caught ball in his hands; your character can break and be rendered incapable of taking possession of the ball; the ball can frequently become uninteractable for brief periods of time, etc.

These things are often attributed to the dev team being poorly equipped to handle such issues, their previous experience being in a single-player only game. This is understandable, and could be forgiven if not for the deliberate choices made by the devs (or perhaps the publisher?) that are incontrovertibly unacceptable. 

BY FAR, the single MOST EGREGIOUS offense of this game is charging $30 for it as if it is a completed "buy-and-play" title, only to use a "Free-to-play" battlepass monetization model. It also feels deliberately misleading to players who may have played the beta. In beta, everything in the battlepass was easily attainable for free by simply playing the game. Plentiful currency rewards allowed you to unlock each and every reward, making it simply feel like  "player progression" veiled behind a season-esque UI. In the full retail, however, this is no longer possible. At least half of the content for the game you ALREADY PAID FOR is locked behind an additional paywall of in-game currency that you are mathematically unable to acquire enough of without shelling out more real-world cash. This is scummy, downright inexcusable, and is the main reason this review is negative. 

On top of all this there are just so many bugs/glitches. Items in the shop (hairstyles, shirts, etc.) will frequently vanish or be unpurchaseable; visual glitches can discolor certain parts of your player; crashes and disconnects are incredibly frequent; the list goes on. 

I really enjoy the game, it's incredibly fun, and I dislike the idea that this review could be a contributing factor to rematch becoming nothing more than a foot note in the annals of history, but I simply can't justify a positive review with the state things are in currently. I will, of course, continue playing (I've already purchased, after all) and should the devs adequately address the plethora of issues I will gladly change my review to positive. That remains to be seen, however, and unless the free-to-play monetization style in particular is removed, this review will remain negative.

Apesar dos problemas esse é o melhor jogo de futebol que conheço. Não que seja muito dificil, a competição não é muito grande.

 Em questão de diversão e mecânica, esse jogo é fabuloso. Mas no geral tem muitos problemas com bugs e latência na conexão, o que é péssimo pra um jogo competitivo. Fora outros problemas na jogabilidade, principalmente do teclado e mouse.

 Mas no fim das contas é de se esperar que uma empresa de médio porte como a SlopClap teria problemas pra deixar o jogo liso, eu acredito até que eles estão lidando muito bem com esses problemas. As pessoas criticam a empresa como se ela fosse uma daquelas grandes empresas multi milionárias que exploram seus jogos e jogadores, como se em 4 semanas já fosse possível arrumar todos os problemas do jogo sem ter um exército de programadores. Esses caras acham que fazer um jogo é fácil, logo a demora que ta tendo é única e exclusivamente porque a empresa não está dando ouvidos a comunidades ou sei lá o que.

 Quando o jogo lançou eu fiquei preocupado que eles começassem a focar em lançar cosméticos a cada semana pra farmar o máximo de dinheiro possível a curto prazo, como todos os últimos jogos competitivos de empresas pequenas que eu joguei (todos faliram). Graças a Deus alguém lá tem um cérebro e as decisões parecem ir em direção à deixar o jogo polido para o competitivo o mais rápido possível e resolver os principais problemas apontados pela comunidade, pelo menos de acordo com o que eles falam em seus pronunciamentos.

 Torço pra que eles estejam sendo transparente quanto ao desenvolvimento do jogo, e se isso for verdade Rematch terá um grande futuro. Mas no momento é ter paciência e manter reportando à empresa dos problemas.

I really want to like this game. I've willed myself through dropping it at least 4 separate times, but it's just not sticking. I haven't played it a lot as of this review, and I think that's for good reasons.

The quality of the connection to the match and other players is lacking. Those in the fighting game community are probably familiar with things like rollback and desync, where what you see isn't actually reality, and your client will rapidly correct itself to match what's canon on the servers. In a game about controlling a ball, this is a problem. You can momentarily appear to have perfectly dribbled through the defenders, only to have your client update and see that someone tackled and stole it from you. You can be perfectly lined up with defender stance and appear to catch the ball as a goalie, only for your client to update and it turns out you were just a hair too late to save it.

The community, at least in quick play, seems OK. Perhaps even better than average for online games. Far more often than not, when someone does use voice chat, they're trying to coordinate or being extremely positive in general. Still, it's annoying and even stings sometimes when you make a mistake only to watch someone spam "Thanks!", or even leave the game entirely. I've seen clips of players crashing out and generally acting more like capital G Gamers in ranked mode, but as I've only just barely unlocked the mode  myself, I can't say I've experienced it. YMMV.

The controls are just a bit awkward, regardless of whether you choose mkb or controller. "With modifier" comes the closest to being intuitive, but I like having dedicated hotkeys for moves in a game like this, so I erred on the side of trying to make "without modifier" work. Why doesn't light tap get its own bind? Lob light tap does. Why can't you bind "Call ball" to C, at all? (It always reports a conflict, no matter what your other buttons are set to.). Why is goalie pushball a separate bind from standard pushball? There's always some sort of compromise made that stops just short of feeling natural.

Lastly, in a competitive sense, making it so you can't actively derank until a certain rank floor functionally converts that rank to the lowest rank in the game. It feels bad. Players that earned that rank through skill and a high win rate don't want to play with the players that got that same rank through perseverance and hundreds of games at a much lower winrate.

The practice mode drills are a good idea. Unfortunately, actually drilling them is tedious. Once you complete one, the game is stuck in some form of tutorial loop, so it jettisons you back to the menu so you can select the next tutorial in the series. There's also not really any good drill for practicing your 1v1s or shots in a meaningful way. I do have to give props to the ball control drills, I just wish the course was more rapidly repeatable. It would also be nice, as an alternative, to have accessible bot matches or customizable training modes. Maybe even some form of single player campaign?

I don't think the devs are going to be able to address all of the issues the game has before community interest dries up. If the question is, "why play this game that doesn't work quite right yet and is in dire need of a single player mode, more robust training tools, and more, when other games are out that work well and include everything you need to learn right now?", I don't have an answer to that.

⚽ Rematch est une pépite pour tout amateur de foot et de fun multijoueur.
Ce jeu, c’est un mélange parfait entre arcade, stratégie et pur délire. Chaque match est une nouvelle histoire, pleine de retournements de situation et de buts incroyables. La prise en main est rapide, mais la profondeur de gameplay est réelle — plus tu joues, plus tu veux t’améliorer.

💡 Les + :

Gameplay ultra fluide, nerveux et addictif

Animations et physique de balle très bien fichues

Ambiance sonore et visuelle qui met dans le mood direct

Des moments épiques entre potes, avec des cris, des rages, des fous rires

Mode en ligne stable, matchmaking rapide

Pas besoin d’être un pro du foot pour kiffer

🛠️ Devs à l’écoute, mises à jour régulières et une vraie passion derrière le projet. C’est rare et ça fait plaisir !

🎯 En bref : si t’aimes le foot, les jeux compétitifs funs, ou juste te marrer avec tes potes en hurlant “C’EST LE REMATCH !!!”, fonce. C’est un must-have dans ta bibliothèque Steam.

🔥 10/10, je ne joue plus qu’à ça.

[h1] Blue Screen Twice Just Trying to Load Up Game! [/h1]

A friend of mine kept asking me to get this game. I didn't really want to, but I gave in because this friend and I barely have any games we play together. 

The first time I load up the game, it tells me the game cannot launch and needs to be closed and reopened. I clicked the orange button to do so, but a few seconds after, my monitor flashed blue and Windows said there was a security error. After loading back up my computer, my bluetooth was hidden in my device manager and would not active for Windows 11 to use. 

I searched the internet and found this was a common issue with Rematch. The game causing many players to have blue screens or crashing. I suspect this has something to do with the Anti-cheat. Maybe the game thinks my Bluetooth wireless KingKong Gullikit controller is cheating, or my JBL speaker? I have no clue. 

After finally getting my Bluetooth back on, seeing as it had be disabled in my BIOS, I came back and tried to relaunch the game. Again, my monitor flashed blue with messaging from Windows. My monitor restarted and Bluetooth was disabled once more. 

I am going to seek a refund for this game, and I will not touch it until the blue screen crashing is fixed. 

Thank you for your time, and I hope the developers address this soon.

J'aimerais énormément recommander ce jeu, étant fan de foot et de sport en général ça fait vraiment plaisir d'avoir enfin un nouveau jeu de foot en multi qui se réinvente, mais bon sang Rematch a tellement de soucis
Premièrement les serveurs en papier mâché, je sais pas si c'est à cause du nombre de joueurs dépassant les attentes des devs ou à cause d'Anti-Cheat de merde mais c'est impossible de faire une seule partie sans souci de rollback, sans décalage avec le jeu ou sans bug/crash/déconnexion/etc. C'est insupportable et 100x pire que dans les premières bêtas. Y'a rien de plus frustrant que de perdre à cause d'autre chose que la faute des joueurs, ici à cause du jeu en lui-même. Le poste de gardien est injouable avec tous les soucis de serveurs et de balle qui part dans tous les sens, qui se marque alors qu'elle est dans nos mains, etc.
Ensuite, et ça c'est un peu moins la faute des devs mais bon sang les joueurs sont tellement nuls, apprenez à faire la dernière passe devant les cages, ça ne vous tuera pas. Il n'y a pas de meilleure façon pour avancer sur le terrain qu'avec des passes rapides, mais les gens préfèrent essayer de dribbler seul et perdre la balle comme des idiots, ou foncer jusqu'aux cages pour tirer droit dans les mains du gardien. 
Troisièmement, pour un jeu qui se veut un minimum compétitif je remarque qu'il manque énormément d'éléments qu'on retrouve dans les jeux comme ça et qui complètent le système : Je pense par exemple à un vrai système de signalement pour signaler les joueurs qui font exprès de perdre, qui insultent en vocal ou qui sont AFK/qui se déconnectent, un système d'"Honneur/Fairplay/jsp", c-a-d un système de points qui punit les gens qui se déconnectent ou qui se font signaler à juste titre, et pour citer un dernier exemple l'option d'abandonner un match en cours sans avoir à se déconnecter : quand 3 de mes coéquipiers rage-quit, j'ai pas spécialement envie de jouer le reste de la partie en 2v5 donc une option pour FF serait appréciée
De manière générale ce jeu manque cruellement de polissage et souffre en prime d'une playerbase bête comme ses pieds

O jogo é um beta que custa 90 reais.

Sistema de matchmaking não existe, você cai aleatoriamente com pessoas de qualquer elo, o que frequentemente te coloca contra um time fechado de 5 adolescentes com 500h de jogo e no seu time um cara que tem 5h. O jogo tem um modo 'ranked' mas sem nenhum suporte pra isso, não existe report, matchmaking, seu elo não dropa abaixo da divisão 3, se alguém kitar não existe remake ou cancelamento da partida, são comuns partidas 5x4 ou 5x3 onde o time lesado não pode fazer nada além de esperar o tempo passar ou levar 4 gols. 
 
Netcode faz o COD Ghosts do lançamento parecer um sonho. A bola some, teletransporta, aparece atrás, na frente, em qualquer lugar. O sistema que decide a prioridade de quem deve dominar a bola é confuso e não funcional, parece que o jogo joga uma moeda pra decidir quem vai chutar primeiro em uma disputa.

MUITOS bugs que se tornaram 'feature' e são abusados de maneira extensiva pelos jogadores.

Comprando esse jogo você paga pra jogar uma versão beta: tudo pela metade, tudo mal feito. Parece até que tinham um "jogo" minimamente funcional e decidiram lançar assim mesmo.

Aguarde mais uns 2 anos pra jogar um jogo minimamente funcional, afinal os desenvolvedores anunciaram que vão levar de 6 a 9 semanas pra adicionar a feature de report (então imagine o resto).

✅ Un concept original et addictif, très bon jeu en équipe avec un système de stats détaillé qui donne envie de progresser. L’aspect compétitif est bien pensé, et les parties sont dynamiques.

❌ Mais il manque deux options essentielles pour améliorer l’expérience :

Une sélection de rôle (notamment gardien) pour éviter les conflits ou les compositions bancales en début de match.

Une option pour abandonner une partie quand l’équipe est en sous-effectif ou qu’un joueur troll, au lieu de devoir subir jusqu’à la fin.

🔧 Ces ajouts rendraient le jeu bien plus stratégique et agréable pour les joueurs sérieux.

En l’état, je recommande, mais avec une grosse marge de progression côté ergonomie.

👍 Note : 7.5/10 en attendant les mises à jour.

1 aydır tek başıma ara ara oynuyorum, plat 3 de hard stuck kaldım. 5 kişilik takımla bir gecede elite'e yükseldim. Kısaca 5 kişilik takımınız yoksa bu oyuna hiç bulaşmayın derim. Kanserlik bakımından  LoL'den hiçbir farkı yok. Yanlış tek bir hareketinizde size oyun seslerini spamlıyorlar gerekirse pas atmıyorlar kısacası sizi oyundan soğutacak herşeyi yaparlar. Ayrıca klavye ve fare ile oynuyorsanız mekanikler ara ara sapıtıyor. Ya ben casual olarak oynarım achievement yaparım falan deseniz achievementları bile düzgün çalışmıyor. Yahu kardeşim oyunda kaleci olarak asist yap achievementını koymuşlar, steamin forumlarına girdim baktım herkes kafasına göre procluyor, bazen oluyor bazen olmuyor diyor. 40'tan fazla kaleden asist yaptım yok bir türlü alamadım. Kafasına göre proclayan achievement mı olur amk?! Oyunda 50 ms'in üzerine çıktığında millet ışınlanmaya başlıyor ki genellikle çıkılıyor. Oyundan rastgele insanlar sürekli oyundan kopuyor, hemen bağlanıyım desen onuda yapamıyorsun. Oyunda kimlik doğrulama hatası denen s2k bir olay var, sırf bu yüzden neredeyse her seferinde oyundan tamamen çıkman ve tekrar bağlanman gerekiyor. Oyun maç başı zaten 6 dakika. Girdin çıktın maç bitiyor. Oynamayın, oynatmayın, rematch pişmanlıktır.

Seit Rocket League mal ein Spiel, wo ich mich hätte sehen können, hunderte von Stunden zu versenken, Ranked zu spielen, ein Team zu starten und Spaß zu haben.

Also Spiel ist early Access und Cashgrab!

Coming from Rocket League I was so pumped for this and to be able to have a bigger squad with more friends.  As I wouldn't call this game perfect yet, I'd say it's so damn fun and addicting when you start playing with a squad.  Most times if you go in solo or even just picking up a solo random it'll likely be a ball chasing idiot who doesn't know what the pass button is or that the best plays are passing plays and they'll shoot it from half field.  Other than that aspect I'd say there's still a little bit of polish they need to put on it, as well as find something to get players a little more hooked because even though it's a blast, it's definitely not the Rocket League Killer I was thinking it'd be.

+ Хорошая задумка дворового футбола. Матчи длятся не долго, игру ищет быстро.

- Нет системы репортов или другой системы для борьбы с людьми, специально портящими игру. Нет даже элементарной блокировки игрока, чтобы в последующих играх не играть с ним.
- Визуальные баги связанные с неткодом, иногда вы просто не узнаете, что вы уже не владеете мячом, так как на сервере у вас его отобрали 2 секунды назад, или постоянные отскакивания мяча от ворот в руки вратарю.
- Нестабильность в работе серверов, игроков то и дело периодически выкидывает на несколько секунд, человек может банально поймать разрыв соединения с сервером стоя на воротах, либо находясь в атаке у ворот.
- Игровой магазин постоянно ломается, то цены крестиками, то просто не работает.
- Один раз мяч перестал быть активным, из-за чего никто не мог ничего сделать и игра шла бесконечно.

p.s. Если вратарь ловит мяч и улетает за линию ворот, это не является голом, вы серьезно?!

- Начнем с самого вкусного "Ранкед режим 5х5": 
Играть 5х5 невозможно, поскольку тебя закидывает к Пакистано-Турецко-Индусским мальчикам, которые живут на краю цивилизации и буквально перемещаются на телепорте по карте вместе с мячом, эти **бланы в патч ноуте пишут, что боятся добавить ранкед 3х3 потому что игроки рассосутся по разным режимам. Уберите свою 5х5 помойку, если вы не умеете в неткод

- Отсутствие системы репортов :
Играя в замечательный ранкед 5х5 в 1 из 3 игр независимо от рейтинга тебе попадется тело, которое расплакается с 0 и начнет гриферить в свои ворота, эта дура будет отнимать мяч, мешать тебе и другим и пробивать в свои ворота, из-за чего играть в соло в режиме 5х5 ПРОСТО НЕРЕАЛЬНО, рематч же конечно знали, что у каждого "футбольного любителя" есть еще 4 друга извращенца, которые несомненно согласятся играть вместе с ним (клоуны я ваши рты накручивал)

the greatest football game ever

I grew up in Scotland, where football was forcefully injected into my life ever since I was a wee lad - and to be honest, I HATED it. I was forced to go to games and watch (boring), I was forced to watch it on TV (boring), I was forced to play it all the time at school and with friends (I wasn't bad at it, but again, boring), and it just never was a sport for me. I hated how all the players take any chance to dive like a pre-madona to get the ref to punish them, I much preferred other sports like hockey where they get ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ clobbered but just get back up and go - and if there's an issue, they fight it the ♥♥♥♥ out. But again, football is such a staple in Europe as a general it doesn't matter where you go, It's just there and everyone loved it. Despite my hatred for football (or soccer as ya'll yankies and NA folks call it), this game slaps. I have so much fun with it, and I never thought a football game would actually grip me like this has. It's just.....fun! I'm over 30 hours in and still just go back too it a lot - sure it has some issues, net code and weird teleportation of whats actually happening with the balls sometimes, but once you click with this game, it's so so so much fun.

Thanks for listening to my TED talk about I hate football but like this game. Highly recommend, especially if you have friends to play it with - communication is key!

Amazing base game, it's gonna get so much better as time goes on, but it's already something I will spend hundreds of hours on. Great skill ceiling while incorporating fantastic team play options. When you feint the opponent with a dribble, hit some quick passes with your teammates or just a clean curve shot faking out the goalie, it gives the same feeling as beating a late game boss in a Dark Souls game. 

The only cons I can think of right now is:
- Somewhat sluggish servers which sometimes can result in the ball teleporting between players close to each other, assuming it's cause they both got control of the ball on their screens. Or in the worst cases some players disconnects/crashes. It's never happened to me even tho I'm rocking a 9 yo I7-6700K but it's something you do see once or twice every 10 games or so.

- Only 5v5 ranked. It can be a struggle cause team play is really important and with 4 other people to rely on it's a bigger chance that one or more teammate is not gonna be compatible with your play style. Having at least one or two friends with you in a 5v5 can really up the odds of a win.

- Some QoL missing like replay options in the training workshop modes (it's a time drain to be sent to the main menu everytime). The queue for a game is sometimes not cancelling when you try, especially rematching after a game. Been so many times that the queue rolls on no matter when you press the button to cancel. Also need more options for the in-game quick chat. But it's nothing big and hopefully obvious for Sloclap and an easy implementation after the first rounds of balancing patches.

TL;DR A great game with little to be picky about even now. I guess some people are having crashes/disconnects sometimes but it hasn't been my experience. Fully recommend anyone that enjoys competitive team focused games to try it out, but it also has a multitude of options for egoists to shine!

Nota: 8/10 — e seria 10 se o goleiro não perdesse disputa de bola aérea com o vento.

O jogo é MUITO divertido e altamente viciante.

⚽ Pontos positivos:

O principal mérito é que o jogo te joga direto pra dentro de um anime. Tem hora que tô no ataque, meto um voleio bonito e me sinto o Goenji em Super Onze usando o Furação de Fogo (Fire Tornado) ou o Nagi em Blue Lock. Quando tô na zaga e tiro a bola da área com uma bicicleta, já me acho o Oliver Aiku. Só falta o narrador gritando meu nome com a main theme de Blue Lock tocando de fundo.

Outro ponto sensacional: não tem skills nem builds apelonas. Aqui, ou você joga bem porque treinou, Isso deixa o jogo justo e competitivo de verdade. Quando você aprende uma mecânica nova e manda bem, sente que foi por mérito seu, não porque equipou o "Super Chute +3000 Roubadasso de Fogo das Galaxias".

E, diferente dos modos carreira do FIFA e do PES, aqui eu realmente me sinto parte da partida. Mesmo sem história, tem uma imersão absurda — talvez pela câmera ou pela jogabilidade (que tens seus defeitos de polimento, mas é boa).

🤬 Pontos negativos:

Infelizmente, a comunidade do jogo às vezes parece uma reunião dos piores personagens de anime... sem enredo.

Tem o cara que acha que é o Isagi reencarnado e tenta chutar de primeira toda bola que encosta. Tem o Bachira da deepweb, que tenta driblar o time inteiro e perde a bola no primeiro toque. E o clássico: o tiltado, que é ruim demais, erra tudo, e ainda fica spammando “mandou bem” no chat toda vez que o time leva um gol — geralmente porque ele fez uma cagada e entregou a bola de presente.

E claro, os jogadores que quitam porque o time tá perdendo. Amigo, você acha que é um protagonista de anime? nem no anime o protagonista abandona o jogo. Fica e aprende alguma coisa com a derrota, cacete.

Agora, falando dos problemas do jogo em si:

    A conexão é um mistério da natureza. Às vezes você tá voando em campo, e do nada: pum, desconectado. Parabéns, você foi driblado pelo próprio servidor.

    Sistema de prioridade na bola? Só se for prioridade pros bugs. Você acerta o tackle, o jogo diz que contou, mas a bola continua com o adversário. Como? Magia? Código mal assombrado?

    Disputas aéreas são uma piada. O goleiro sai com as mãos, mas perde a jogada como se tivesse tentado cabecear com o nariz.

    Domínio de bola? Só se for "domínio perdido". O dominio de bola desse jogo é o oposto do domínio do Nagi, o jogador domina a bola jogando ela a dois metros pra frente, e o adversário agradece o presente.

💡 Sugestões de melhoria:

    Adicionem partidas com bots, por favor. Nem todo mundo quer passar raiva com o Isagi genérico online todo dia.

    Implementem um sistema de denúncia. Tem muita gente babaca que não sabe jogar e fica enchendo o saco.

    E uma sugestão de coração: façam uma parceria com Blue Lock! Imagina que foda jogar com skins do Nagi, Isagi, Rin… Campos inspirados no anime, uniformes... ia ser lendário.

Resumo:
É um jogo com alma, emoção e anime vibes. Já é ótimo, mas com um pouco mais de polimento (e menos lag e instabilidade de servidor), pode se tornar um jogo épico.

Se você gosta de futebol, anime (principalmente Blue Lock) e não se importa com jogadores que se acham protagonistas de anime, esse jogo é pra você.

8/10.

Mon Avis Négatif sur le Jeu Rematch

Après plusieurs heures passées sur Rematch, je ressens une réelle frustration.
 Le jeu souffre de nombreux bugs récurrents qui ruinent complètement l’expérience de jeu. Qu’il s’agisse de crashs intempestifs, de problèmes de connexion ou de fonctionnalités qui ne répondent pas, il est difficile de profiter pleinement du gameplay.

En plus de ça, l’optimisation laisse franchement à désirer : même sur une machine récente, j’ai constaté des ralentissements, des chutes de FPS et des temps de chargement anormalement longs. Cela me donne l’impression que le jeu n’a pas été suffisamment peaufiné avant sa sortie.

Mais ce qui me dérange le plus, c’est le manque de transparence des développeurs. Les retours de la communauté semblent ignorés ou balayés d’un revers de main, sans réelle communication sur les corrections à venir ni sur la feuille de route du projet. Au final, j’ai bien du mal à recommander Rematch dans l’état actuel des choses.

Кушать — еда, пинать — мяч. Вижу шар — делаю Хуяч.
Годный симулятор синей тюрьмы. Вас закрывают с инвалидами в количестве от 3 до 5, где каждый борется за право владения мячом. Зачастую игра быстро переходит из командного соревнования в дезматч режим во всех проявлениях животного эгоизма. Выживает не тот, кто лучше пинает мяч, а тот, кто быстрее осознаёт: союзники - это лишь временное неудобство на пути к личной победе. Обезумевший хаос в перемешку с проявлениями низменной человеческой природы, которыми можно насладиться особенно играя в пати. Однозначно рекомендую! Вступай в 3-ю штурмовую, стань бомбардиром!

Même si il s'agit d'un foot "fiction" orienté five, on retrouve quelques sensations et concept très proche du véritable football et c'est sincèrement bien fichu, cependant.
Ils doivent améliorer le jeu qui est encore trop bug, dont des bugs qui handicap vraiment (bug de manette, de lag, de déco incompréhensible).
Surtout pour un jeu qui veux s'orienter classé / E-sport

Il a le potentiel d'être un gros jeu de cette scène, mais faut paufiner le tout.

Un gros problème du jeu ne vient pas des devs en eux même mais de la mentalité des joueurs en ligne.
Alors, c'est classique et commun vous me direz, mais par son gameplay + la culture foot, c'est très exacerbé chez les joueurs de Rematch.
Un exemple évident étant le rainbow flick, qui est dans le vrai foot un "geste technique spectaculaire" (qu'on voit assez peu en vrai match, pas pour rien) mais qui ici s'execute extrêmement facilement et peut bien fonctionner à très bas rank.
Très facile à contrer, les joueurs se reposent beaucoup dessus, avec derrière cette volonté de "briller", de faire des "compil youtube", plutôt que de jouer vraiment au football avec la passe et le jeu sans ballon.

Pour ça que je recommande FORTEMENT de jouer avec des amis et de leur expliquer comment ne pas tomber dans ces pièges.
Et là, le plaisir est énorme ! 

Mais en solo tag, c'est très rapidement l'enfer et vous ne pourrez pas forcément jouer comme vous avez envie, parce que vous devrez compenser les errances et les erreurs de vos adversaires.
Pour climb haut plat / diamant, on peut presque y arriver uniquement en assurant l'équilibre défensif et en jouant simple.

[h1]🎮 REMATCH: Quando o Futebol Encontra a Nova Geração do Competitivo[/h1]

Prepare-se para esquecer tudo o que você conhece sobre jogos de futebol.
[b]REMATCH[/b] não é só mais um jogo esportivo – é um [i]marco[/i] na forma como sentimos, jogamos e vivemos o futebol digital.

Desenvolvido pela [b]Sloclap[/b] (de [i]Sifu[/i] e [i]Absolver[/i]), o jogo entrega uma experiência [u]absurdamente fluida, responsiva e envolvente[/u], que coloca o controle da bola e da vitória [b]literalmente nas suas mãos[/b].

[hr]

[h2]🚀 Inovador, Ágil e Viciante[/h2]

REMATCH propõe partidas intensas em arenas fechadas, sem impedimentos, sem faltas, sem enrolação. Só futebol puro, técnico e brutalmente honesto.

[b]Principais mecânicas:[/b]

⚡ [i]Unreal Engine 5[/i] com animações cinematográficas e zero delay

🎯 Controle individual em partidas 3v3 até 5v5

🤸‍♂️ Rabonas, bicicletas, jogadas plásticas e sistema de precisão real

🧠 Stamina, sprint, defesa e finalização com peso real nas decisões

[hr]

[h2]🤝 Sloclap: A Empresa Que Ouve[/h2]

Se existe uma palavra para definir a Sloclap: [b]respeito ao jogador[/b].
A cada patch, nota-se claramente que os devs estão [u]escutando a comunidade[/u], respondendo dúvidas, aplicando melhorias solicitadas e atualizando com consistência.

Crossplay já confirmado para updates futuros

Feedback sendo considerado inclusive para modos offline

Correções rápidas em bugs e aprimoramentos de matchmaking

[b]É raro ver um estúdio com essa transparência.[/b]

[hr]

[h2]🏆 Competição Oficial? Temos também.[/h2]

Não é só diversão. REMATCH é também uma plataforma para jogadores competitivos.
[b]A Rematch Official[/b] e a [b]Federation[/b] estão organizando torneios, rankings e campeonatos semanais com estrutura de eSport real.

🥇 Premiações em dinheiro e reconhecimento nacional/internacional

🌍 Partidas transmitidas ao vivo e cobertura de destaques

🧑‍💻 Drafts de jogadores, torneios por região e estrutura profissional

[i]Se você joga sério, esse é o seu lugar.[/i]

[hr]

[h2]🔥 Por que você PRECISA jogar?[/h2]

[b]✅ Pontos Fortes:[/b]

Jogabilidade fluida, responsiva e técnica

Visual estilizado com arenas imersivas

Recompensa para habilidade e trabalho em equipe real

Totalmente focado em experiência competitiva online

Comunidade ativa, engajada e respeitada

Atualizações frequentes com base em feedback

[hr]

[h2]🎯 Veredito Final[/h2]

[b]REMATCH[/b] é uma revolução no gênero de esportes.
Mistura a essência do [i]futebol de rua técnico[/i] com o [i]combate preciso de um jogo de luta[/i]. Tudo isso com estilo, carisma e propósito.

[b]Nota final: 5/5[/b]
[i]Indicado para squads, jogadores solo competitivos, fãs de Rocket League e quem busca algo novo, rápido e viciante.[/i]

Im absolutly amazed by the game. Its very fun to play and the learning curve is super visible. But I still think many things are lacking:
Many AFKs, that just go unpunished in the elo system and the players with the AFK player should get less LP loss,pretty much like in League of Legends, since in 1/5 games theres an AFK. Despite people saying there are many bugs, I have not encountered many. I think a "try again" button in the tutorial is ABSOLUTLY NECESSARY. Its very frustrating trying to practice and having to search for 8sec every time, so that is my biggest wish. I mainly play goalie, and often I feel like the commands are very unmooth, maybe some control in the air would be cool like a jump forward could be divided by a high and low jump, which could be done with the "square button" for PS controllers, since It is still unused which is a shame. I really like the Voice chat, but maybe it can be made so u can mute single players and not mute the full team. So one player screaming for example cant sabotage the experience for the others that want to listen to the voice chat.

Thats it from me, higly recommend this game and hope it gets big. Love to the developers <3

Game has horrible tech support and constantly crashes.

10/21/25 update
Over 200 hours at this point and I really had hopped after all this time I could change this game to "positive" review.
Here we are though and the game simply has not improved enough. The servers are Janky as they ever were, high pings for no reason, people teleporting, seemingly no sense made for how the ball ends up, etc....but honestly at this point I've grown use to that, I'm pretty confident they will never fix the servers either from lack of priorities or money...who knows. The problem I have these days is the ranked gameplay....dear lord its painful...even if you have a couple friends, the probability that you will get newbies is crazy. Have climbed to diamond twice now and even though despite solo queuing in diamond I get some of the most god awful players you've ever seen....theres no way they are diamond. Missing simple concepts, bad movement, etc....They are currently pointing fingers at how you can't drop leagues or don't gain points based on MMR as the reason and next patch is supposed to change this. I doubt this will be the case, pretty sure its the tiny pool of remaining players and what ever "MMR" match making they are doing is very lose, if its even basing anything on MMR at all....thats what makes it feel so bad to play right now plain and simple. All the bugs, glitches, server issues to the side....its the fact that you're team mates will always be (no matter how high you climb) be a mix match of newbies to people your rank makes it to painful. I'm officially only going to jump on this when my friends feel like playing......good job screwing the pooch on this one sloclap...

TLDR; Stay far far away from this, unless you want to be frustrated a developer who has no idea what they're doing
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

8/4/25 update
100+ hours to date, 40+ over last two weeks.
So their last patch (7/23) made some UI improvements...thats about it for the positive...

Not sure what they did to the netcode but they made it worse some how? I see what we call de-sync issues more then ever now(you can kick the ball but can't "take control" so you can't goalie or dribble...you know pretty core skills) nothing worse then trying to do your rank up matches and this happens to one of your team mates or you...
Also another fun issue where the ball likes to "rubber band", the best I can describe this is imagine you kick/push the ball it goes one direction then flies around your feet in 5 different directions for the next second or two....
I could go on but yea....you get the idea.... 
These aren't rare issues either....i play with 7 other people and they confirm this as well across their matches...

Once again...I stress this game has so much potential...but they don't seem to be putting all the effort into what matters the most(spoiler alert its not crossplay) but this netcode that makes the game feel bad too often...

The numbers show this....checked the steam count today and they are dipping lower and lower...more people played Age of empires 2 definitive edition today then rematch....

I'm sure crossplay will probably bring a surge of players for a bit...but if they don't fix the netcode the game will die slowly but surely....

Ill go down with the ship, too invested now....so heres hoping they fix it sooner rather then later

(PS might as well not fix the permanent "first win of day" achievement showing after every match, its pretty much a meme for us now)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Original review:

Oh boy....So much good, so much bad....So lets break it down into those two lists
GOOD
1. When playing with friends its a blast
2. Controls are pretty simple to pick up but mastering will take time for sure
3. There's enough soccer and "not" soccer to make it fun without it being just another soccer game(passing and good fundamental soccer skills are a must but you can do silly things like slide tackle without punishment and bounce the ball off all the out of bounds invisible walls(which adds more strats.
4. Multiple sizes of matches 3v3, 4v4, 5v5.
5. Built in 4 point mercy rule so need to suffer if you getting owned



BAD
1. Servers...oh gawd the servers they have improved some but there is still random de-syncing where you can't pick up the ball or people do a short teleport, ball movement doesn't make sense, good ol' fashion server lag, etc.
2. Playing solo is a pretty bad time, if its not long Qs at times its just team mates doing whatever or trying to "bluelock" it...if you know you know...
3. Only 5v5 ladder atm nothing else for competitive.
4. Sometimes things just won't work....randomly won't let me choose another banner when customizing my character, won't let me choose my colors at the beginning, voice chat randomly turning itself back on when i boot the game up, the fact that Ive gotten the "first win of the day award" for every match I've ever played(I don't think its giving me any extra points, just pops up every time)...which leads me into....
5. Fixes? Please? - Theres been patches...and servers have improved some but some of the simple things like the "first win of the day" have continued since launch. They sure found time to add a 10 dollar skin to the store but can't fix day one issues. What are you all doing? This isn't a early access game its a FULL RELEASE game that we had to pay for, why aren't these things being addressed?
6. No place to check server status.
7. Have to rejoin custom match after every game if you want to change up the teams. I have 6 friends I play this game with, so sometimes we play 3v3 against each other, there is no way currently to just go back to the custom lobby and change up teams, you have to recreate it again....I mean really?!

BOTTOM LINE:
When you are able to log in with a few friends and get lucky with server roulette(how stable your server is, once again it has gotten better but still happening too often imo) this game is fantastic and I can play match after match after match. All other times when the server lags, random stuff breaks, or you don't have anyone to log in with, its  a mid if not frustrating experience. If they could fix some of the basic stuff and focus on making the net-code/servers better, this game will have some legs for sure. I'm afraid though that they are probably working mostly on crossplay under the assumption that will keep their numbers up but I'm really afraid they are going to push it out and introduce a bunch of new issues. That last bit is just a guess of course. 
Hate that we don't have a option in between thumbs up and down(come on steam not all games are crap or great) but in its current state and their lack of fixing basic things it makes me worry for the future of the game and I have to give it the ol thumbs down despite how much fun I have playing with my friends.

(I didn't mention cosmetics because I don't feel they are good or bad, there's a 10 dollar season pass and you do get a lot of options just none of them are very impressive tbh...."Oh boy i got slightly different cleats" or "Yay i now how a clown wig!" also be ready to see a lot of vitiligo, if you don't know what that is oh boy are you going to know after rematch)

Rematch is a fun and promising game that offers a solid foundation for something truly great. The gameplay is engaging and shows clear creativity, making it easy to spend hours exploring and experimenting. There’s a certain charm in its style and mechanics that makes it stand out from similar titles.

However, the game isn’t without its flaws. Some animation glitches and awkward cutscenes can break immersion at times, and performance issues occasionally impact the overall experience. Optimization is clearly still a work in progress, with noticeable frame drops or loading delays in some areas.

Despite these setbacks, Rematch has a lot of potential. With further updates and polish, it could easily become a standout title. If the developers manage to fix the technical issues and refine the animations and cinematic sequences, this game could go from good to exceptional.

Jogo tem uma ideia boa, mas totalmente bugado.
Se você entra em uma fila para achar o jogo e tentar cancelar, não funciona. Precisa dar Alt+F4 antes de achar o jogo.

Muitas mecânicas do jogo estão mal polidas, desarmes estranhos, bolas defensáveis simplesmente atravessam o goleiro, o atacante que chutar um cruzamento parece ter sempre uma prioridade de animação pulando mais alto, enfim.
Dá pra perceber que o jogo ta longe de estar pronto, mas mesmo assim ele te cobra 90 reais pra jogar.
O engraçado que a loja dentro do jogo funciona sem bug e é atualizada frequentemente rsrsrs.
O jogo é novo e tem potencial, vou torcer para Sloclap resolver esses problemas que frustam totalmente a experiencia do jogo.

Nach 100 Stunden auf der Uhr müsste man eigentlich zu dem Entschluss kommen, dass das Game alles richtig macht. Macht es aber leider (noch) nicht. 
Die Torhüter sind in vielen Situationen viel zu OP. Andererseits kann man die z.B. die Höhe der Parade nicht bestimmen. So hat man nur Einfluss auf die Richtung in die man springt.
Die Mitspieler sind häufig nur darauf aus, sich zu Tode zu dribbeln und denken nicht daran zu passen, sich frei zu laufen oder mal mit nach hinten zu arbeiten.
Generell ist das Matchmaking noch extrem verbesserungswürdig. Man bekommt selten mal ne gute Truppe zugeteilt, häufig wie gesagt nur Einzelspieler. Diese sind auch ziemlich toxisch unterwegs und kommentieren jegliche Fehlaktion mit einem zynischen: "Gut gespielt!". 
Das Ranking ist ebenfalls noch ziemlicher Käse. Ich bin am Anfang direkt auf Gold eingestuft worden, relativ schnell auf Platin gekommen und hänge dort nun seit gefühlt 80 Stunden fest. Man müsste für einen Sieg halt etwas mehr EP bekommen als für eine Niederlage. So wechselt man fröhlich immer zwischen den Divisionen und macht keinen Progress. Denn selten erlebt man eine Siegesserie von 4-5 Spielen oder mehr. Bei vielen Spielern fragt man sich, wie die in Platin laden konnten.
Wenigstens kann man aus der jeweiligen Klasse nicht mehr absteigen.

Ansonsten sind es Kleinigkeiten, dass man z.B. eine Kadereinladung nur sieht, wenn man ins Hauptmenu wechselt. So kann es passieren, dass man immer direkt nach einer neuen Partie sucht und 30 Minuten gar nicht ins Hauptmenu schaut.
Der Netcode ist an manchen Stellen auch noch etwas wackelig auf den Beinen und Ragequitter werden nicht bestraft, ausser dass sie solange nicht mehr nach einer Partie suchen können, wie die Verlassene noch anhält.
Der Shop und die Items sind ziemlich mäh und der "Season Pass" bietet viel zu wenig Inhalt. Ich bin jetzt Level 128 und kann mir im Endeffekt nichts dafür kaufen. Im wahrsten Sinne des Wortes.

Gut, das Game ist noch nicht lange draußen und ich bin sicher es wird sich noch gut entwickeln. Dafür hat es zu viel Potential und macht einfach süchtig.

Update nach 180 Stunden:
Das Spiel ist kaputt. Man bekommt nur noch toxische Spieler, die einen Egotrip nach dem anderen fahren. Es kommen keine Patche von den Entwicklern, die mal was am Matchmaking und vor allem am Rankingsystem ändern. Nur 5 vs5 ranked nervt einfach nur noch. Der Shop ist ein Witz. Ich weiß gar nicht mehr wohin mit meiner InGame Kohle und Token. Die nächsten 34 Seasons kann ich direkt alle damit einlösen. Der Netzcode wird immer schlechter. Von meinem 243er Rang kann ich mir gar nichts kaufen und ansonsten hört man von den Entwickler gar nichts mehr. Es ist im jetzigen Zustand zum Scheitern verurteilt. Hier muss schleunigst etwas passieren.
Ach übrigens: Ich bin immer noch Rang Platin. Und solange man solche Mates zugelost bekommt wird sich daran auch in den nächsten 180 Stunden nichts ändern. Schade, weil das Game eigentlich Bock macht.

Game is fun at times when you are playing with a five stack but even then you are fighting the games network issues and bugs. Of course it is two different departments at work but its sad to see new cosmetics come in while you have bugs and other assorted issues go unsolved. The ranking system is the laziest excuse for a system I have seen in a long time. You have a scoring system for participation and such yet none of this goes in the ranking points you receive or lose; it is always either gain twenty RP(rank points) or lose seventeen or eighteen rp. There is also no reputation system so you can have people griefing your games, spamming slurs, disconnecting and nothing happens to anyone score gained or reputation as a player. I do love this game visually and the grounded game mechanics; I love the different fields and the music queues really feed into your immersion into your game. This is just my opinion but the game should make you complete all the training courses and such before you are able to touch the ranked mode. I thoroughly enjoyed playing through them and continue to play them when warming up for a few games. I hope maybe within the next year or so we will have a great polished game but until then I can't give this game a recommendation.
Would only recommend playing casually with friends. Too many bugs in this state to be taken seriously.

The game is really good, and playing it reminds me of the time i started Rocket League almost ten years ago. I even bought the season pass to support the team, but i can't continue playing this game in this state. Between the lag, the servers and most importantly the random freezes when i launch the game that force me to do a hard reset of my computer, that's enough, i've had enough. This game needs a couple more months of development because it still has to fix a few things. Sometimes it feels like I'm still playing the beta version that was available a few months ago.
There’s also a bug when you're the goalkeeper — sometimes you grab the ball, and other times your character does the animation but it still ends up being a goal.
There are a couple of weird techniques that let you move the ball to one side and then go the other way, and it literally teleports the ball to your feet, letting you get past the entire defense. In one match, a guy from the other team just kept doing that the whole game, and they only won because of that. It wasn’t for anything else — they didn’t even have good aim.
I know this is more about the community, but everyone thinks they’re the main character and that they can dribble through the whole other team. Even Messi knew he couldn’t do it alone, and these dudes think they’re good. It’s actually funny watching them try to take on everyone when they lose the ball after the second touch.
PS: you’ll have to defend alone 85% of the time.
And this one’s not that bad, but I think it’s a controller thing — sometimes when you shoot the ball and you tilt the joystick just a bit too much, the ball goes way off to that side. But maybe that’s just a skill issue, I guess.
I’ll probably keep playing — it’s all fun and jokes if you’ve got friends, but they seriously need to fix some stuff.

Edit:
It's crazy how this game still has the same issues it had the first time I played. Nothing has changed — the bad mechanics, people thinking they're Messi, all the same stuff as always.
And honestly, it’s sad to see that the game hasn’t improved at all in the last few months. The devs only seem to care about random collabs no one asked for and stuffing the store with more items. There’s so much that needs fixing: half the time you intercept the ball and the game doesn’t even register it, the servers are trash, and it keeps stuttering non-stop.
I really hope this game becomes what was promised someday… but at this point, I’m not sure that day will ever come.
